what_it_does: Records a design argument that the project's typed, directional, multi-scheme address edges are fundamentally incompatible with Obsidian's untyped, vault-local, path-based wikilinks, and that bridging them is not free.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id item-24374392, source claude_code, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-48cd6801
Title marks the item as P7 · 2.1 and HIGH priority
Body is a skeptical, bullet-structured argument with bold emphasis

Contrasts the project's edge model (typed, directional, resolved through one resolver across 19 address schemes) with Obsidian's wikilink model (untyped, bidirectional-by-accident, filename/title match only)
Lists address schemes such as code://, board://, image://, decision://, session://
Claims Obsidian's graph view, backlinks, and unresolved-link detection see almost none of the actual typed relationships
Warns that denormalizing typed edges into body wikilinks would duplicate the source of truth, lose the kind, and create a third writer of edges
Warns that Obsidian would flag board:// and code:// targets as unresolved links, tempting creation of junk notes
standouts: The central claim that typed address-edges and Obsidian wikilinks 'do not compose' and the gap is permanent, not a one-time migration
The assertion that Obsidian's headline graph/backlinks features largely do not apply to the project's data as stored
The warning that a sync layer would become a dangerous third writer of edges
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, history
Uses markdown heading and bullet argumentation
Uses internal URI-style addresses (board://, code://, etc.)
Breaks neutral tone by taking a skeptical, emphatic stance and using loaded language such as 'misleading' and 'corrupting'
concerns: Assumes Obsidian's built-in resolver is the only possible resolver, without considering plugins, custom URI handlers, or API extensions
The 'almost none' and '19 schemes' claims are asserted without supporting evidence in the file
The duplication and third-writer risks are hypothetical; no prototype or concrete sync design is shown
No alternative integration approach is proposed; the file is purely a rejection
notes: A skeptical design note arguing that Obsidian's wikilink model cannot faithfully represent the project's typed, multi-scheme address edges.
questions: What are the 19 address schemes and where is the resolver/registry defined?
Is there an existing or planned Obsidian plugin or custom URI handler that could resolve board:// and code:// links?
What do 'P7' and '2.1' refer to in the title?
What is item-48cd6801 and how does this item relate to it?
Has the denormalization/sync approach been prototyped, or is it entirely hypothetical?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a high-priority design argument documenting the claimed incompatibility between typed address-edges and Obsidian wikilinks.
apparent_themes: data model fidelity over tool convenience
risk of false positives corrupting data
intention_signals: It appears intended to block or discourage a naive Obsidian integration by showing that the link models are structurally mismatched and that Obsidian's graph features would misrepresent the real ledger.
novelty: It frames the integration problem as a permanent ontological mismatch rather than a migration or formatting issue, and explicitly counts a sync layer as a dangerous third writer of edges.
contribution: It contributes a precise articulation of the structural differences between typed directional address-edges and untitled bidirectional wikilinks, plus a concrete risk inventory (duplication, loss of relation kind, false-positive unresolved links) that any integration design must address.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item arguing that the project's typed, directional, multi-scheme address edges cannot be faithfully mapped to Obsidian's untyped, vault-local, path-based wikilinks, so Obsidian's graph/backlinks features would misrepresent the ledger and any sync would introduce edge-author conflicts.
intention_for_embedding: To record a skeptical design position that a naive Obsidian bridge would lose relation kinds, duplicate edges, and generate misleading unresolved-link noise.
